attack_name: Frontend R7 UEH To FPT Comparison Loop

core_desire.content: user-facing product impact plus economic mobility

ai_stance.content: leverage for research, prototypes, and market analysis

location_vision.content: Vietnam base with international or remote option later

risk_philosophy.content: moderate risk with practical university runway

key_quote.content: I want a practical route where portfolio, internship, and school network reinforce each other.

autonomy_level.content: partial autonomy, later product lead or small-team path

skill_targets.content: UX research, product thinking, business analytics, basic frontend/no-code, English presentation

portfolio_goal.content: 2-3 product/UX case studies plus internship-ready proof

credential_needed.content: degree for network and signaling, portfolio for proof

role_category.content: product coordination / UX research / business analysis

company_stage.content: small product team or startup with a real product

day_to_day.content: talking to users, writing requirements, testing prototypes or no-code solutions, reading basic data, and turning real problems into case studies

autonomy_level.content: self-directed but constrained by client/team scope, requirements, and delivery realities

required_skills_coverage.content: covers business plus system/data foundations with project artifacts, user/product thinking, and room for basic technical work; a strong bridge for Product BA/UX research and product analyst paths, provided the school curriculum actually includes evaluated projects rather than theory-only lectures

stage_reasoning.uni:
UEH MIS remains unproven on the decisive artifact-fit issue. The only surfaced official-looking evidence is a single UEH course outline snippet showing a presentation/report component for Management Information Systems worth 20 percent, plus discussion/exercises and attendance. That is not enough to establish a curriculum-wide requirement for repeated assessed artifacts such as reports, dashboards, process/system analyses, case studies, or company-linked internship/project outputs. The broader official sources surfaced only generic curriculum/assessment navigation and a graduate thesis/dean project guide, which do not yet demonstrate the undergraduate MIS path forces students to build a sustained artifact chain. Therefore the provisional verdict still holds: UEH cannot yet be treated as top 1 on artifact-building fit; the gap remains unresolved until more official program, course outline, or graduation/project requirement evidence is found.
PROBE: target_school - UEH MIS artifact-fit claim vs weak official evidence: the curriculum still lacks proof of repeated assessed reports, dashboards, process/system analyses, case studies, or real-data/company-linked projects. Compare FPT next using official curriculum, project, and internship evidence; if FPT has a stronger mandatory artifact chain than UEH, temporarily rank FPT above UEH, then compare RMIT and UEL.

uni_research.contradiction_to_test: UEH MIS/HTTTQL should only stay top 1 if official curriculum, course outlines, or graduation/project requirements show mandatory assessed artifacts such as reports, dashboards, process/system analysis, case studies, or data-based/company-linked projects; if the evidence remains limited to a few small assignments or one-off project components, the artifact gap is not resolved.
uni_research.search_query: UEH HTTTQL de cuong hoc phan bao cao thuyet trinh de an mon hoc thuc tap doanh nghiep do an tot nghiep
uni_research.evidence_summary: UEH evidence summary from frontend R6: one UEH course-outline result mentioned a presentation/report component for Management Information Systems worth 20 percent plus discussion/exercises/attendance. Other surfaced sources were generic curriculum/assessment navigation or graduate thesis/project guidance. This is weak evidence for a repeated undergraduate artifact chain, so UEH is only conditional for artifact-first Product BA/UX fit.

user_tag.self_authorship: The student owns the comparison rule: artifact evidence first, then internship/network, international exposure, and ROI.
user_tag.core_tension_reasoning: UEH is only conditional unless official evidence shows a repeated artifact chain; FPT should become the temporary top school if it has stronger curriculum/project/internship proof.

uni_message.content: If we compare schools, UEH is only conditional unless the curriculum shows repeated artifact production. Which criterion should I inspect first?

Soi artifact trong curriculum truoc. Neu UEH MIS chi co vai mon ly thuyet va mot do an nho thi chua du. Em muon bang chung ve mon hoc, do an, report, dashboard, case study, internship/project voi doanh nghiep neu co. Sau do moi can network/quoc te/ROI.
UEH is still conditional. Should I check course list or assessment/project outputs first?

Chon dau ra do an va hinh thuc danh gia truoc. Em can biet co mon nao bat sinh vien lam report, dashboard, phan tich quy trinh/he thong, case study hoac project voi du lieu thuc khong. Neu khong co bang chung ro thi tam thoi UEH khong phai top 1, hay so qua FPT/RMIT/UEL.
I will choose the source myself. If UEH only has one or two small reports and no repeated artifact chain, should I treat that as not enough?

Artifact la cot uu tien mot roi, dung hoi lai cot nua. Tam thoi UEH la conditional neu bang chung chi co vai report nho. Hay so sang FPT truoc theo official curriculum/project/internship evidence; neu FPT manh hon thi chuyen top tam thoi sang FPT, sau do moi so RMIT va UEL.

_expect.expected_behavior: Use FPT evidence to make a provisional FPT-vs-UEH ranking move or proceed to the next named comparison school; do not ask another meta-threshold question.
